attacking_style: Willing to commit to ultra-long solo breakaways (100km+), showing track racing aggression translated to road racing. Goes early rather than waiting for decisive moments.
team_tactics: Appears to operate with freedom for individual breakaway attempts rather than being deployed as a domestique. Limited evidence of coordinated team support.
terrain_preference: Track specialist background suggests suitability for tempo-controlled stages and time trials rather than sustained mountainous breakaways where mechanicals and endurance become critical.
vulnerability: Mechanical reliability issues have derailed promising breakaway efforts. Sustainability over 100km+ solo efforts questionable despite willingness to commit. KOM jersey ambitions undermined by equipment failures.
key_patterns: Commits to extremely long solo breakaways exceeding 100km (2 observations)
Mechanical issues have ended promising efforts, suggesting equipment or positioning vulnerabilities (2 observations)
Targets KOM classification through early, sustained breakaway presence rather than tactical climbing (2 observations)
form_trajectory: unknown: Both observations from same race week affected by mechanicals, making true form assessment impossible
